q: Which adaptation of xylem vessels directly supports the movement of water?
options: Xylem cells remain alive to actively pump water
Xylem cells are lignified, dead, and have no end walls, forming hollow tubes
Xylem vessels have sieve plates to filter large molecules
Xylem contains companion cells to provide energy for water transport
explanation: Xylem vessels are dead, hollow tubes reinforced with lignin; the absence of end walls allows unobstructed flow of water from root to leaf.
